title: ETS concurrent access — read/write concurrency
purpose: By default, ETS tables are protected by a single RwLock. The `{read_concurrency, true}` and `{write_concurrency, true}` options enable fine-grained concurrency for tables with skewed read/write patterns. Read concurrency optimizes for many readers by using read-biased locks. Write concurrency uses striped locks (sharding) to allow concurrent writes to different keys.
task: Implement the `read_concurrency` and `write_concurrency` table options with appropriate locking strategies.

requirements.title: read_concurrency option parsing
acceptance: For DashMap-based tables (set/bag): no-op (DashMap already supports concurrent reads)
For ordered_set: switches from Mutex<BTreeMap> to RwLock<BTreeMap> allowing concurrent reads
Test: create table with read_concurrency, multiple concurrent readers don't block

requirements.title: write_concurrency for set/bag tables only
requirements.spec: write_concurrency is supported for set and bag table types (DashMap is already sharded). For ordered_set, write_concurrency is NOT supported in this brief — hash-based sharding would break the global key ordering required by first/next/last/prev traversal. A concurrent ordered data structure (skip list or concurrent B-tree) is a future optimization.
acceptance: `{write_concurrency, true}` parsed and applied for set/bag tables (no-op — DashMap already sharded)
`{write_concurrency, true}` on ordered_set is accepted but logged as ignored (single-lock behavior retained)
ordered_set first/next/last/prev iteration continues to work correctly
Test: set table with write_concurrency — concurrent writes succeed
Test: ordered_set with write_concurrency — iteration order preserved

requirements.title: Concurrency option interaction
acceptance: Both options can be set simultaneously: `[ordered_set, {read_concurrency, true}, {write_concurrency, true}]`
Default (no options): single lock per table
read_concurrency without write_concurrency: readers don't block each other, writers are exclusive
write_concurrency without read_concurrency: writers to different shards don't block, readers may block
Test: verify option combinations produce expected behavior under concurrent load

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement `{write_concurrency, auto}` (OTP 25+ auto-tuning) — use explicit boolean only
SHALL NOT change the DashMap-based set implementation (DashMap already provides sharded write concurrency)
SHALL focus on ordered_set which currently uses a single Mutex<BTreeMap>
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
